reasoning: Initial observation suggests a linear relationship between position, velocity, and time. To confirm this, I will increase the time and observe the change in position.
Increasing time did not change the position since the initial position was already set to 0.0. This supports the linear relationship between position, velocity, and time.

The position increased by 10 meters when the initial position was increased by 10 meters, while velocity and time remained constant. This supports the equation position = initial_position + velocity * time.
